mission.title: Install and Configure Cloudevents on Kubernetes
mission.description: CloudEvents is a specification for describing event data in common formats to provide interoperability across services, platforms, and systems. Installing CloudEvents allows you to standardize event data representation across your applications and services.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the CloudEvents Helm repository to your local Helm configuration using the following command:
```bash
helm repo add cloudevents https://gitlab.com/api/v4/projects/15775100/packages/helm/stable
```

steps.title: Update Helm repositories
steps.description:
Update your Helm repositories to ensure you have the latest information:
```bash
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install CloudEvents
steps.description:
Install the CloudEvents Helm chart in a new namespace called 'cloudevents':
```bash
helm install cloudevents cloudevents/cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --create-namespace --version 0.13.1
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the CloudEvents pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cloudevents
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the CloudEvents deployment:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CloudEvents release using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall cloudevents --namespace cloudevents
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) associated with CloudEvents:
```bash
kubectl delete crd cloudevents.cloudevents.io
```

uninstall.title: Delete the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'cloudevents' namespace to clean up all resources:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace cloudevents
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, backup the current state of your CloudEvents installation:
```bash
helm get all cloudevents --namespace cloudevents > cloudevents-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to get the latest chart information:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade CloudEvents
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the CloudEvents release to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade cloudevents cloudevents/cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --version 0.13.1
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the CloudEvents pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cloudevents
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending
troubleshooting.description:
If the CloudEvents pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources or node issues:
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace cloudevents
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are in CrashLoopBackOff, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cloudevents
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the CloudEvents service is not reachable, ensure the service is correctly configured:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cloudevents
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of CloudEvents will result in the CloudEvents pods running without issues in the 'cloudevents' namespace. You should be able to access and interact with the CloudEvents service as intended.
codeSnippets: kubectl set resources deployment cloudevents --namespace cloudevents --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
